summary: This is a very brief news post linking to Part Four of a series about FogBugz development. The sole substantive claim is a striking statistic: only 2% of Fog Creek's total effort goes into writing new code that ships to customers. The post uses this dramatic ratio to draw readers into the longer article about the realities of software product development.
key_points: Only 2 out of every 100 calories of effort at Fog Creek go toward writing new shipping code
The vast majority of software development effort is consumed by activities other than writing new code
The post serves as a teaser linking to a deeper exploration of where development effort actually goes
money_quotes: Out of every 100 calories expended by the Fog Creek team, just 2 calories are spent on actually writing new lines of code that ship to a customer.
key_insight: Writing new code that ships is a tiny fraction of the total effort required to develop and maintain a software product.
